hypothesis: In a reactive carbon electrolyzer operated at the paper’s champion conditions (1.5 M KHCO3/0.75 M K2CO3, 3 M K+, 54°C, 144 mL min−1 KHCO3 flow, 2.4 M KOH at 24 mL min−1), replacing constant current with a 1 Hz square-wave between 0 and 350 mA cm−2 at a 0.71 duty cycle (average 249 mA cm−2) will increase reactor yield by at least 20% relative to the best constant-current operation at the same average current density.
